abstract: This review deconstructs particle–interface–electrode integration to analyze limiting factors and evaluate enhancement strategies of kinetics for Si anodes.

title: Achieving 20% efficiency in binary organic solar cells with suppressed non-radiative recombination via triphenylamine halides
abstract: A series of high fluorescence triphenylamine halides are proposed as solid additives to reduce the static disorder and thus improve the luminescence performance of the active layer to suppress non-radiative recombination loss.

title: Molecular engineering of residual lithium compounds for stable LiNi 0.92 Co 0.05 Mn 0.03 O 2 cathodes
abstract: Rationally-designed 2,5-thiophenediylbisboronic acid reacts with residual lithium compounds, followed by in situ electrochemical conversion, to form a stable cathode electrolyte interface that improves lattice integrity and battery performance.

title: Biocrust-inspired interface layer with dual functions towards highly reversible zinc metal anodes
abstract: Inspired by the biocrust in desert ecology, we used eflornithine additive to in situ construct a ZnF 2 -rich SEI with crust effect on zinc anodes, which suppressed the dendrite growth and side reactions on an aqueous zinc-ion battery and stabilized it.

title: All-day freshwater and power generation via integrated photothermally enhanced thermoelectrics and evaporative cooling
abstract: This work presents an all-day continuous power-freshwater generator through enhanced solar heating and evaporative cooling. Scalable series units provide independent, zero-carbon resources for off-grid communities.

title: Operando monitoring of gassing dynamics in lithium-ion batteries with optical fiber photothermal spectroscopy
abstract: Gas evolution is an inherent aspect in batteries. We present an optical fiber spectroscopic technique for operando gas sensing, enabling comprehensive mechanistic studies in batteries and broader electrochemical energy systems.

title: Suppressing non-radiative recombination for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: This review analyzes non-radiative recombination mechanisms, device stability, and hysteresis, providing strategies to reduce trap states and improve the efficiency and stability of perovskite solar cells, offering a forward-looking perspective.

title: Synergistically enhancing the selective adsorption for crystal planes to regulate the (002)-texture preferred Zn deposition via supramolecular host–guest units
abstract: Supramolecular host–guest units synergistically enhance the selectivity and adsorption ability for Zn crystal planes, facilitating homogeneous Zn(002) deposition at high current densities and areal capacities.

title: Synergetic insights into Nb single atoms and lithiophilic support for high-efficiency sulfur catalysis in Li–S batteries
abstract: A surface-active Li + assisted LiPS conversion strategy is proposed. The *Li + -rich catalysts can accelerate LiPSs conversion through changing the Li + attack pathway.

title: Constructing a dual-fiber network in high efficiency organic solar cells via additive-induced supramolecular interactions with both donor and acceptor
abstract: All additives ( TZ-Cl , TZ-2Cl , TZ-3Cl ) can facilitate the formation of a bicontinuous network structure in active layer through supramolecular interactions, and TZ-3Cl achieved PCEs of 19.8% and 20.2% in binary/ternary OSCs.

title: Arbitrary and active colouring of solar cells with negligible loss of efficiency
abstract: Self-assembled cholesteric liquid crystals uniquely enable non-spectral coloured and thermochromic covered highly efficient photovoltaic devices for aesthetic integration.

title: Modeling single-crystal electrodes as a network of primary particles
abstract: A physics-based network model of primary particles, informed by tomography, predicts LiFePO 4 electrode behavior across conditions without empirical tuning, bridging particle-scale physics to cell performance.

title: Phase homogeneity mediated charge-carrier balance in two-step-method halide perovskite photovoltaics
abstract: Phase homogeneity mediated charge-carrier balance reveals the heterogeneity issue of two-step-method perovskite films.

title: An oriented design of a π-conjugated polymer framework for high-performance solid-state lithium batteries
abstract: An oriented π-conjugated polymer host design decouples ionic conductivity and mechanical strength, enhancing performance for solid-state lithium metal batteries.

title: Low-cost, resilient, and non-flammable rechargeable Fe-ion batteries with scalable fabrication and long cycle life
abstract: Fe-ion batteries durably deliver 225 mA h g −1 for 27 000 cycles, offering non-flammable, cost-effective energy storage solutions using innovative PANI/CNT cathodes and carbon steel anodes, along with theoretical insights into working mechanisms.

title: Payback trade-offs from the electrolyte design between energy efficiency and lifespan in zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The payback period is a critical indicator when adopting energy storage systems. When developing optimization strategies for emerging energy storage technologies such as aqueous zinc-ion batteries, their economic feasibility should be considered.

title: Highly reversible zinc anode enabled by a trace-amount additive with pH buffering capability
abstract: Trace-amount additive (10 mM, 0.1 wt%), ammonium succinate, was introduced to endow electrolyte with excellent pH buffering ability, resulting in high CE (99.91% and 99%) at low current density (1 mA cm −2 and 0.5 mA cm −2 ) and high cycle stability.

title: Heterostructure conductive interface and melt-penetration-bonding process to afford all-solid-state Li–FeF 3 garnet batteries with high cathode loading
abstract: A hot melt-penetration-bonding process of ionic wires is developed to address the issues of high contact impedance at cathode/garnet interface and insufficient conduction in bulk cathode.

title: The thermal runaway mechanism of fast-charging lithium-ion batteries using LiFSI-based electrolytes
abstract: The thermal runaway mechanism of LiFSI-based batteries is firstly revealed in which tremendous heat is released due to N 2 generated via decomposition of LiFSI. Quenching N-containing free radicals is put forward to improve battery safety.

title: Molecular polarity regulation of polybromide complexes for high-performance low-temperature zinc–bromine flow batteries
abstract: Polybromide molecular polarity regulation ensures the optimal performance of zinc–bromine flow batteries at room temperature and −20 °C.

title: Atomic-level insights for engineering interfacial hydrogen microenvironments of metal-based catalysts for alkaline hydrogen electrocatalysis
abstract: An understanding of the intrinsic relationship between defined atomic properties of metal-based electrocatalysts and interfacial hydrogen microenvironments in alkaline HOR/HER is provided.

title: Hydration equilibrium-controlled cation–anion coordination competition for precise recovery of all valuable metals from spent lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The valuable metals in spent LIBs can be precisely separated by controlling the temperature and H 2 O content in deep eutectic solvents.

title: Correction: Ultrathin cellulosic gel electrolytes with a gradient hydropenic interface for stable, high-energy and flexible zinc batteries
abstract: Correction for ‘Ultrathin cellulosic gel electrolytes with a gradient hydropenic interface for stable, high-energy and flexible zinc batteries’ by Jichao Zhai et al. , Energy Environ. Sci. , 2025, 18 , 4241–4250, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5EE00158G.

title: Biomimetic bone hydrogel enables a seamless interface for aqueous battery and human/machine interaction
abstract: An ultra-dense biomimetic bone hydrogel with a seamless interfacial structure was designed for aqueous batteries and on-skin monitoring systems.

title: Simultaneously improving efficiency, stability and intrinsic stretchability of organic photovoltaic films via molecular toughening
abstract: A tethered molecule is introduced to realize intrinsically stretchable organic photovoltaics (OPVs) with over 15% efficiency via benign solvents, and thickness-dependent mechanical properties is elucidated for the first time in OPVs.

title: Crystalline nitrogen-doped-carbon anchored well-dispersed Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticles for real-time scalable neutral H 2 O 2 electrosynthesis
abstract: Crystalline nitrogen-doped-carbon anchored Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticles were synthesized through the pyrolysis of a mixture of g-C 3 N 4 and Fe@Tpy, which promoted scalable and affordable neutral H 2 O 2 electrosynthesis under H 2 O (salt-free) and O 2 condition.

title: Buried interface modification for high performance and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: Phenylhydrazinium chloride (PC) is used as the buried interface modification material of a perovskite film to passivate defects and retard the crystallization process of perovskite films, resulting in improved PCE from 24.67% to 25.80%.

title: Operando observing hydrogen evolution in commercial lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The time-resolved H 2 evolution mechanism in the central void of the jelly roll in a battery during high C rate charging/discharging, including reversible adsorption–desorption, irreversible formation, and a negative temperature coefficient behaviour.

title: A single ion conductive “plasticine-like” solid electrolyte combined with a modulated d-band center of interfacial zinc atoms for highly reversible zinc metal anodes
abstract: We develop a “plasticine-like” solid electrolyte, which shows a high Zn 2+ ion transfer number of 0.9 and contributes to an anti-catalytic indium-rich solid electrolyte interphase by modulating the d-band center of zinc atoms.

title: Dual modulation of coordination asymmetry and curvature unlocks record-level Zn–air battery performance with Fe–N–C single-atom catalysts
abstract: Dual modulation strategy of coordination asymmetry and curvature optimizes the atomic Fe sites, unlocking record-level quasi-solid-state Zn–air battery performance with a capacity of 1.56 Ah and cycling over 2000 h at −40 °C.

title: Ultra-uniform interfacial matrix via high-temperature thermal shock for long-cycle stability cathodes of sodium-ion batteries
abstract: We developed an innovative high-temperature shock (HTS) technique to synthesize uniformly coated materials, resulting in enhanced surface structures, improved cycling stability, and pouch cells retaining over 70% capacity after 700 cycles.

title: Eutectic electrolytes: a new platform for high-safety batteries
abstract: This review elucidates the components, formation and properties of eutectic electrolytes (EEs) in characterization and simulation, highlighting intrinsic EEs and composite electrolytes for large-scale energy storage.

title: Singlet oxygen is not the source of ethylene carbonate degradation in nickel-rich Li-ion cells
abstract: Photochemical assessment shows ethylene carbonate does not react with singlet oxygen, indicating degradation within nickel-rich cells follows an alternative route.

title: Breathing air into water: dual-pathway H 2 O 2 synthesis via aerating amphiphilic supramolecular films
abstract: Amphiphilic PDIOH films enrich and photoreduce O 2 at hydrophobic interfaces, enabling dual-pathway H 2 O 2 synthesis via triphasic “air-to-water” mass transfer at static air–liquid interfaces.

title: Correction: Sodium cluster-driven safety concerns of sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Correction for ‘Sodium cluster-driven safety concerns of sodium-ion batteries’ by Jiaping Niu et al. , Energy Environ. Sci. , 2025, 18 , 2474–2484, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4EE05509H.

title: Halogen-substituted phenazine cores reduce energy losses and optimize carrier dynamics in tethered acceptors for 19.8% efficient and stable polymer solar cells
abstract: The thermodynamic relaxation and rigidity of small-molecule acceptors drive an oligomeric design approach in polymer solar cells, achieving device efficiencies approaching 20% through tailored molecular engineering.

title: Developing low-resistance ion migration pathways using perfluorinated chain-decorated COFs for enhanced performance in zinc batteries
abstract: Superhydrophobic fluorine chains was incorporated into covalent organic frameworks to engineer nanochannels that facilitate fast dehydration of ions and create low resistance ion migration pathways.

title: Rational catalyst layer design enables tailored transport channels for efficient CO 2 electrochemical reduction to multi-carbon products
abstract: CO 2 and H 2 O channels are incorporated into the catalyst layer network to benefit the micro-environment in MEA. Through rational catalyst layer design, CO 2 -to-C 2+ performance is substantially enhanced.

title: Mitigating the amorphization of perovskite layers by using atomic layer deposition of alumina
abstract: Schematic depiction of spiro-OMeTAD degradation causing perovskite instability under pseudo-operating conditions and its prevention using an ultra-thin ALD layer of alumina.

title: Amorphous anion skeletons induce rapid and cation-selective ion flux towards stable aqueous zinc–iodine batteries
abstract: Amorphous anion skeletons of zeolite-like Na 2 Zn 2 (TeO 3 ) 3 induce rapid and cation-selective ion flux towards stable aqueous zinc–iodine batteries.

title: Interfacial ionic and thermal regulation for highly reversible and ultra-reliable zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Mechanically robust and thermally stable separators with enhanced ion regulation for ultra-reliable zinc-ion batteries.

title: Simulating solid electrolyte interphase formation spanning 10 8 time scales with an atomically informed phase-field model
abstract: The solid electrolyte interphase (SEI), formed by electrolyte reduction, governs the reversibility of batteries, however the competition between Li-ion diffusion and reaction kinetics remains poorly understood.

title: Design of strong and weak intermolecular interactions to engineer buried interfaces in inverted wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells
abstract: Low-contact-loss and durable buried interface was engineering by a molecular hybridization strategy. The proton transfer from Me-4PACz to Histamine enables distinguished efficiency and operational stability of PSCs based on mix-halide perovskites.

title: Manipulating anion solvation competitiveness via a multifunctional additive toward robust low-temperature sodium metal batteries
abstract: Enhanced anion solvation competitiveness strategy is proposed to simultaneously optimize Na + -desolvation energetics and direct interphase self-assembling for robust low-temperature SMBs.

title: Scalable synthesis of amorphous NiFe oxide hollow microspheres via glucose-mediated spray pyrolysis for industrial hydrogen production
abstract: Amorphous NiFeO x hollow microspheres via Fe modulation, enabling industrial-scale oxygen evolution at ultralow overpotentials. Scalable synthesis bridges atomic design with electrolyzers, outperforming noble metals through hierarchical porosity.

title: Bimolecular amine vapor passivation for efficient perovskite solar cells based on blade-coated FAPbI 3
abstract: A bimolecular amine vapor passivation (BAVP) strategy enables 25.2% efficient blade-coated FAPbI 3 solar cells with outstanding thermal stability.

title: Biomass-derived functional additive for highly efficient and stable lead halide perovskite solar cells with built-in lead immobilisation
abstract: The functional biomass additive TBA-Alg simultaneously improves the PCE, stability and lead immobility of lead halide perovskite solar cells.

title: Multifunctional additives with dynamic sacrificial S–S bonds for building self-assembled monolayers of Zn-ion batteries with improved stability and longevity
abstract: The BHED additive optimizing the Zn 2+ solvation structure and in situ building the β-ME SAM with highly active zincophilic sites on the Zn anode surface, Zn-ion batteries exhibit excellent interfacial stability, cyclic reversibility, and longevity.

title: Toward practical lithium metal batteries via a solvation structure regulation strategy in in situ polymerized fluorinated gel polymer electrolytes
abstract: Backbone –CF 3 groups non-coordinatively reshape Li + solvation in a gel polymer electrolyte (GPE), balancing interfacial stability and ion transport toward practical lithium metal batteries under elevated-voltage operation.

title: Buried and bulk synergistic engineering enables high-performance inverted 2D/3D perovskite solar cells
abstract: A buried and bulk synergistic strategy was developed to improve perovskite film quality. Optimizing the buried interface using FuMACl and bulk using (DFP) 2 PbI 4 seeds led to a champion photovoltaic efficiency of 26.03% and a fill factor of 86.79%.

title: Supercapatteries: unlocking the potential of battery-supercapacitor fusion
abstract: Supercapatteries represent the ultimate coexistence of high power and high energy density in a single energy storage device. This review highlights the importance of quantitative criteria that differentiate supercapatteries from hybrid capacitors.

title: Highly efficient all-small-molecule organic solar cells with excellent operational stability and blend-thickness tolerance
abstract: Highly efficient ternary all-SMOSCs are developed by a donor alloy strategy, which deliver a record-high PCE of 18.51%. They also exhibit excellent operational stability and blend-thickness tolerance.

title: Efficient organic solar cells with a printed p–i–n stack enabled by an azeotrope-processed self-assembled monolayer
abstract: Azeotrope-assisted self-assembled monolayer printing boosts efficiency across all slot-die coated p–i–n organic solar cells.

title: Decoupling first-cycle capacity loss mechanisms in sulfide solid-state batteries
abstract: Quantified capacity loss mechanisms via atypically low voltages reveals solid electrolyte oxidation reversibility and surprisingly high first-cycle coulombic efficiency.

title: Inhibiting cathode dissolution and shuttling of V–O species using a polybenzimidazole hydrogel electrolyte for durable high-areal-capacity Zn–V 2 O 5 batteries
abstract: The PBI hydrogel electrolyte can reduce the amount of free water, minimize proton co-intercalation into the V 2 O 5 host, and prevent the crosstalk of V 10 O 26 (OH) 2 4− between the anode and cathode to enable durable high-areal-capacity Zn–V 2 O 5 batteries.

title: Designer diffusion media microstructures enhance polymer electrolyte fuel cell performance
abstract: We present a scalable method to manufacture gas diffusion media with tailored microstructures, enhancing polymer electrolyte fuel cell performance and offering a path beyond the traditional carbon fiber paradigm in electrochemical technologies.

title: A microstructural electrochemo-mechanical model of high-nickel composite electrodes towards digital twins to bridge the particle and electrode-level characterizations
abstract: We developed a model that considers all components of a composite electrode. It integrates particle- and electrode-level behavior and enables simulation of the mechanical behavior of polymeric binders, which is critical for battery degradation.

title: Intermolecular interactions triggered crystallization phase transition regulation for efficient and stable perovskite photovoltaics
abstract: The intermolecular interactions triggered crystallization strategy modulates the phase transition process and manages quenching defects of perovskite, enabling broad applicability for high-performance perovskite solar cells and scalable modules.

title: Balanced distribution of donors and acceptors enabled by volatile isomerization additives for 20.81% efficiency layer-by-layer polymer solar cells
abstract: Volatile isomerization additives induce a vertical phase separation morphology with balanced distribution of donor and acceptor as well as excellent carrier dynamics, enabling the maximum efficiency of 20.81% in layer-by-layer polymer solar cells.

title: Dynamic trade-off of electronic structures to activate and stabilize lattice oxygen via a Ce δ + –O/Co–Fe hydroxide interface for industrial level water oxidation
abstract: A CeO 2 /Fe–Co(OH) 2 heterostructure catalyst featuring flexible redox states of Ce δ + as an electronic buffer, enabling dynamic regulation of Co( ii / iii / iv ) and enhancing both activity and stability.

title: Simultaneously improving the efficiencies of organic photovoltaic devices and modules by finely manipulating the aggregation behaviors of Y-series molecules
abstract: We have developed a novel liquid additive that boosts J -aggregation in NFAs through enhanced non-covalent interactions with the BTP core, leading to a record efficiency for thick-film (≥200 nm), large-area OPV modules.

title: Wearing “body armor” on zinc anodes for robust aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The first ultra-strong ANF/CNF interlayer protects zinc anodes from dendrites and side reactions for stable zinc ion batteries.

title: Metastable interphase induced pre-strain compensation enables efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: We developed a pre-strain compensation strategy by introducing a metastable interphase at the buried interface to release the thermal-induced residual tensile strain in perovskite films, leading to highly efficient and stable perovskite solar cells.

title: Crystal phase and band edge modulation of MA- and Br-free CsFA-based perovskites for efficient inverted solar cells and minimodules
abstract: A Lewis base is selected to regulate crystal growth, reduce δ-phase formation and inhibit halide oxidation of perovskites, enabling champion efficiencies of 26.12% for a 0.09-cm 2 inverted device and 22.67% for a 12.96-cm 2 minimodule, respectively.

title: Gas-medium annealing for perovskite solar cells with state-of-the-art energy loss
abstract: Gas-medium annealing simultaneously accomplished crystallization and surface passivation, contributing to high-quality perovskite films with much fewer defects from bulk to surface. A minimized energy loss of 0.29 eV was obtained for efficient PSCs.

title: High thermoelectric conversion through an optimal contribution of electronic carriers in polymeric mixed ionic–electronic conducting films
abstract: An optimal contribution of electronic and ionic carriers in mixed ionic–electronic conductor (MIEC) films was explored to achieve high voltage and power density for waste and solar heat harvesting at a low temperature gradient.

title: Battery lifetime prediction using surface temperature features from early cycle data
abstract: Statistical temperature features from the first 10 cycles were used to develop machine learning models, showing competitive performance across various battery cathodes and operating conditions for early-cycle battery lifetime prognostics.

title: Fluorine-free electrolytes in batteries: principles, strategies, and advances
abstract: Electrolytes play a pivotal role in battery technologies, influencing performance and safety.

title: Freezing the p–i–n interlayer with a symmetric phenolic compound to achieve favorable vertical morphology and efficient pseudo-planar heterojunction organic solar cells with excellent stability
abstract: A strategy of using symmetric phenolic compound 4,4′-dihydroxybiphenyl to freeze p–i–n interlayer of pseudo-planar heterojunction organic solar cells has been first developed, resulting in optimized and stable vertical phase separation morphology.

title: Molecular engineering of d -glucuronamide additive directs (100)-oriented Zn deposition for ultra-stable zinc-ion batteries
abstract: An electrolyte additive d -Glucuronamide ( d -Glu) functionalized with hydroxyl, carbonyl, and amide groups and a 3D architecture is introduced to co-modulate the thermodynamics and kinetics of zinc plating/stripping synergistically.

title: Covalent organic and metal organic frameworks based single atom catalysts for valorisation of CO 2 to value added chemicals
abstract: Schematic overall representation of COF and MOF single atom engineered catalysts for valorization of CO 2 , along with their characterization and theoretical studies.

title: Regulating interfacial reactions through electrolyte chemistry enables an anion-rich interphase for wide-temperature zinc metal batteries
abstract: Zinc-ion batteries are challenged by zinc dendrites, notorious side reactions, and poor performance at low temperatures.

title: Screening thermoelectric materials for high-output performance in wearable electronics
abstract: | S |/ κ and S 2 σ / κ 2 are proposed as more effective indicators than zT for screening thermoelectric materials to develop powerful thermoelectric devices used in the exacting working conditions of wearable electronics and the Internet of Things.

title: Ultrathin cellulosic gel electrolytes with a gradient hydropenic interface for stable, high-energy and flexible zinc batteries
abstract: Ultrathin cellulose-based electrolytes (DCG, 10 μm) with a gradient hydropenic interface were designed to minimize side reactions and guide (002) textured deposition. DCG enhanced flexible Zn batteries to 222 W h kg −1 and 214.3 W h L −1 .

title: A dynamic cathode interlayer for ultralow self-discharge and high iodide utilization in zinc–iodine batteries
abstract: TMA + cations enhance Zn plating behavior and capture polyiodides to form a dynamic cathode interlayer, which suppresses the polyiodide dissolution and involves them back into redox reaction.

title: Oxygen-tuned aluminum-based halide solid electrolytes enabling low-voltage anode compatibility in all-solid-state batteries
abstract: Oxygen-tuned aluminum-based halide solid electrolytes balance reduction stability and ion conductivity, enabling stable cycling with 0.3 V-class alloy anodes and paving the way for high-energy-density all-solid-state batteries.

title: Dissolution swelling effect-assisted interfacial morphology refinement enables high efficiency all-polymer solar cells
abstract: Small molecule incorporation during sequential deposition improved donor crystallization and intercalation, optimizing the heterojunction morphology. The PCEs achieved 19.39% for small-area (0.04 cm 2 ) and 17.71% for large-area (1 cm 2 ) devices.

title: Nitride solid-state electrolytes for all-solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: Nitride solid-state electrolytes offer intrinsic stability with lithium metal and improved ionic conductivity. This review highlights advances in their design, synthesis, and application for enabling practical all-solid-state lithium metal batteries.

title: Integrated modeling for the transition pathway of China's power system
abstract: A temporally and spatially resolved model charts feasible transition pathways of China's power system to negative emissions by 2060 through renewables expansion and carbon capture.

title: Unraveling the electrolyte-free interface in membrane CO 2 electrolysers
abstract: We discuss emerging phenomena in membrane electrode assembly CO 2 electrolysers and highlight the critical role of electrolyte-free interfaces in governing catalytic performance at the device level.

title: Self-assembly-assisted dynamic placement of noble metals selectively on multifunctional carbide supports for alkaline hydrogen electrocatalysis
abstract: Samples obtained via self-assembly-assisted dynamic placement exhibited outstanding mass activity among state-of-the-art AEMWE electrocatalysts.

title: Asymmetric cross-orbital coupling in Fe–Mn spinels decouples structural stability and kinetics in sodium-ion storage
abstract: We show that asymmetric cross-orbital coupling in K x Fe y Mn 1− y O z spinel decouples the trade-off between stability and kinetics. Fe doping induces Mn e g –Fe t 2g synergy, boosting both electronic conductivity and structural integrity.

title: Improved efficiency and stability of outdoor and indoor organic photovoltaics with suppressed voltage loss via alkoxylation on dimeric giant acceptors featured as supramolecular stabilizers
abstract: Alkoxylated dimeric giant acceptor DYO-V boosts organic solar cell efficiency and stability, achieving 20.2% PCE outdoors and 28.1% indoors, offering a versatile strategy for durable, high-performance photovoltaics.

title: Fullerene-embedded porphyrin metallacages as photoactive additives for stable binary organic solar cells with a certified efficiency of 20.2%
abstract: Fullerene-embedded porphyrin metallacages are used as photoactive additives for the first time to manipulate the optoelectronic and morphological properties of binary organic solar cells for achieving a certified efficiency of 20.20%.

title: Opportunities and challenges for emerging inorganic chalcogenide–silicon tandem solar cells
abstract: This review highlights the potential of emerging inorganic chalcogenide–silicon tandem solar cells, which address efficiency and stability limitations of single-junction devices and offer a less-toxic, more stable alternative for tandem applications.

title: Stress-dissipative strong bimodal molecular packing towards efficient and highly stretchable organic photovoltaics
abstract: The Cl-16C additive engineers PM6 packing through inducing multidirectional crystallinity and increasing physical crosslinking, achieving tripled elongation at break with 19.7% OPV efficiency for the PM6/BTP-eC9 system.

title: Exposing binding-favourable facets of perovskites for tandem solar cells
abstract: Controlling the growth of polycrystalline perovskite films to achieve a favourable termination can significantly reduce interfacial losses and enhance the output voltage in wide-bandgap and all-perovskite tandem solar cells.

title: Central core asymmetric acceptor design enables over 20% efficiency in binary organic solar cells by suppressing non-radiative energy loss and optimizing nanomorphology
abstract: Two acceptors Ph-2F and Ph-2Cl are designed via core-asymmetric substitution and halogenated engineering. Ph-2F based organic solar cells (OSCs) achieve an efficiency of 20.33%, which is the highest value for OSCs based on asymmetric acceptors.

title: Dual strain regulated RhNiAu trimetallene for efficient plasmonic-promoted acidic nitrate electroreduction
abstract: Dual strain regulated RhNiAu trimetallene effectively suppresses the competitive hydrogen evolution reaction and accelerates acidic nitrate electroreduction kinetics, while the anchored Au nanocrystal enables plasmon-enhanced catalytic performance.

title: Water electrolysis technologies: the importance of new cell designs and fundamental modelling to guide industrial-scale development
abstract: Advancing water electrolyser technologies through modelling and design innovations to power the future of green hydrogen at gigawatt scales.

title: The impact of current mismatch among individual cells on the performance of perovskite photovoltaic modules
abstract: The distribution of short-circuit current among individual perovskite solar cells significantly affects the performance of large-area modules. To minimize power losses, it is essential to control this variation within 2%.

title: Interfacial phase regulation of flexible single-ion conducting block copolymer electrolytes ensuring ultra-stable lithium metal batteries
abstract: An SIPE with synergistically optimized mechanical properties and ionic conductivity is designed in this work and the transport mechanism of Li + is revealed by MD simulations. The formation of a stable SEI layer promotes uniform Li + deposition.

title: Reviving ether-based electrolytes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The solvation binding between the ether–Na + is weaker than that of the ester and Na + , promoting a faster desolvation of Na + . This advantage positions ether a competitive candidate for enhancing the kinetic performance of SIBs.

title: CsPbI 2 Br quantum dots integration for high performance organic photovoltaics and photodetectors
abstract: Strong chemical interaction between CsPbI 2 Br PQDs and organic semiconductors is revealed, leading to enhanced performance in both organic photovoltaics and organic photodetectors.

title: Corrosion of metallic anodes in aqueous batteries
abstract: This review thoroughly discusses the corrosion behaviors of various metallic anodes in aqueous metal batteries. It also provides a comprehensive overview of corrosion prevention tactics and characterization methods.

title: Designing a bridging solvation structure using recessive solvents for high energy density aqueous zinc-ion batteries with 88% depth of discharge zinc rechargeability
abstract: The recessive solvent is activated by bridging structure, and further self-assemble into nano-capsule solvation, which promotes rapid Zn deposition and generates a uniform SEI, thus achieving a stable Zn anode at high depth of discharge.

title: An enhanced three-stage model for sodium storage in hard carbons
abstract: A multi-technique operando study reveals a three-stage sodium storage mechanism in hard carbon—surface adsorption, accumulation, and pore filling—while classical intercalation is found to be insignificant under practical conditions.

title: Mg 2+ initiated in situ polymerization of dioxolane enabling stable interfaces in solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: A poly(1,3-dioxolane) solid-state electrolyte was via in situ polymerized using Mg 2+ -containing montmorillonite. The competitive coordination of Mg 2+ and TFSI − led to the stable MgF 2 interphases, suppressing the interfacial side reactions.

title: Stability/durability challenges of cathode catalysts for PEM fuel cells: experiments, mechanisms, and perspectives beyond three-electrode systems
abstract: The stability/durability test protocols/parameters of ORR electrocatalysts as well as the degradation mechanisms and solutions to improve catalyst stability/durability are discussed.

title: Self-assembled metal cluster/perovskite catalysts for efficient acidic hydrogen production with an ultra-low overpotential of 62 mV and over 1500 hours of stability at 1 A cm −2
abstract: This work presents an excellent HER catalyst with promising industrial application and reveals the in situ reconstruction processes and formation of active species of Ca 2 CoRuO 6 .

title: Multiscale coupled electron–ion transport in semi-solid lithium flow batteries
abstract: This review provides in-depth understanding of multiscale electron–ion transport in semi-solid LFBs, outlining mechanisms, optimized strategies, multi-physics field modelling, and future directions to bridge fundamental with commercialization.

title: Resting but not idle: unveiling the mechanistic origin of resting losses for zinc anodes
abstract: Through real-time visualization of the spread of spontaneous electron transfer across conductors and in situ operando ECMS, we unveil and quantify the central role of current collector material in resting capacity losses in zinc metal batteries.

title: Intrinsic point defect tolerance in selenium for indoor and tandem photovoltaics
abstract: Intrinsic point defects are found to be inactive for electron–hole recombination, while extrinsic impurities do not contribute significantly to doping, pointing to extended defects and interfaces as limiting factors in selenium solar cells.

title: Enhanced bipolar membranes for durable ampere-level water electrolysis
abstract: The bipolar membranes, designed with innovative catalytic interfaces and advanced membrane layer engineering, demonstrate exceptional long-term durability during ampere-level water electrolysis.

title: Stabilizing the aluminum metal anode through polymer coating-enabled regulation of exchange current
abstract: A strategy to stabilize the aluminum metal anode by achieving a delicate balance between mitigating interphase reactions and ensuring efficient mass transport is proposed and realized through a finely tuned PDMS coating.

title: Breaking the H 2 O dissociation-OH desorption scaling relationship in alkaline hydrogen evolution by oxophilic single atom M 1 –Ru n electrocatalysts
abstract: The oxophilic M 1 –Ru n catalysts facilely overcome the inherent scaling relationship in the alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) over traditional nanoelectrocatalysts, opening up a new opportunity to achieve maximized alkaline HER performance.

title: Levelized cost and carbon intensity of solar hydrogen production via water splitting using a scalable and intrinsically safe photocatalytic Z-scheme raceway system
abstract: Schematic of photocatalytic type 2 Z-scheme raceway design with hydrogen reactor cylinders floating on an oxygen reactor raceway pool. The raceway concept enables a scalable, low-cost, and low-carbon intensity method of hydrogen production.

title: An MBene-based colloidal electrolyte for high depth-of-discharge and energy-density 2 Ah-scale Zn metal batteries
abstract: A hydrated eutectic colloidal electrolyte based on Mo 4/3 B 2 T 2 MBene is developed for Zn metal batteries (ZMBs). The terminal groups of MBene accelerate Zn 2+ transport and anchor I 3 − , ensuring ZMBs with remarkable cyclability under high DOD.

title: Constructing a built-in electric field across an NiMo/NiMoP heterointerface for efficient and durable seawater electrolysis in anion exchange membrane electrolyzers
abstract: Herein, an NiMo/NiMoP heterointerface was developed to achieve both enhanced corrosion resistance and improved electrocatalytic performance in anion exchange membrane (AEM)-based seawater electrolysis.

title: Nature-inspired synergistic strategy: carrier regulation in breakdown self-healing all-organic polymer dielectrics for enhanced high-temperature energy storage
abstract: Through carrier transport inhibition effects of vortex trap and mountain barrier, and gas-condensed phase self-healing mechanism, all-organic polymer dielectric achieves superior high-temperature energy storage and breakdown self-healing properties.

title: Boosting oxygen redox reversibility in chemo-mechanically robust Li-rich oxide cathodes via multi-scale defect design
abstract: This study achieved multiscale defect design by regulating the excess-Li distribution, addressing the asymmetric anion redox reaction and structural distortion in Li-rich oxide cathodes.

title: Comprehensive understanding of the Na 1+ x Zr 2 Si x P 3− x O 12 solid-state electrolyte in advanced sodium metal batteries: a critical review
abstract: All solid-state sodium metal batteries (ASSSMBs) have emerged as promising candidates in large-scale energy storage systems relative to Li/Na-ion batteries using flammable liquid electrolytes.

title: Moisture-inhibited deprotonation at the buried interface enables efficient perovskite solar cells with a high fill factor of over 86%
abstract: The moisture-induced suppression effect is observed in the deprotonation reaction at the buried interface. The deprotonation suppressed solar cell obtains a champion efficiency of 25.69% and an ultra-high fill factor of 86.21%.

title: Sustaining vacancy catalysis via conformal graphene overlays boosts practical Li–S batteries
abstract: A vacancy catalysis sustainer using directly grown graphene mitigates the detrimental coverage on active sites and promotes the preferential decomposition of anions, thereby achieving long-life Li–S batteries.

title: Single-atom tungsten doping induced chemical–electrochemical coupled pathway on Ni(OH) 2 enables efficient urea electrooxidation
abstract: A chemical–electrochemical coupled pathway on Ni(OH) 2 surface has been proposed for high-selectivity urea electrooxidation reaction (UOR) as the alternative of conventional oxygen evolution reaction (OER).

title: Zinc–alcohol–air batteries with ultra-narrow cyclic voltage windows
abstract: Selecting the appropriate charging reaction of zinc–air batteries to reduce cyclic electric energy loss by two orders of magnitude, achieving a round-trip efficiency of up to 99.9%.

title: Iron clusters and single atom sites cooperatively promote bifunctional oxygen reaction activity in ultra-stable flexible zinc–air batteries
abstract: Iron clusters coupled with single atom sites have been developed as bifunctional oxygen reaction electrocatalysts for constructing ultra-stable, flexible zinc–air batteries operable in a temperature range from +40 °C to −40 °C.

title: A Janus-type quasi-solid-state electrolyte enabling dual-ion relay for long lifespan of nonaqueous zinc batteries
abstract: An anhydrous Janus electrolyte with a spontaneously formed ionic double layer enables efficient dual-ion relay conduction, overcoming inherent kinetic barriers in low-salt-concentration solid systems, and delivers unmatched Zn anode stability and full cell durability across a wide temperature range.

title: Deciphering the dynamic solid–liquid interphase for energetic high-mass-loading energy storage
abstract: A dynamic PEI layer was tailored over MnO 2 by refining the cation environment and reconstruction. The unique host-guest coupling restrains Mn species loss and unlocks an energetic “multi-processing” energy storage mechanism.

title: Multifunctional composite magnet realizing record-high transverse thermoelectric generation
abstract: A novel functional material named “multifunctional composite magnet” has been created, which simultaneously exhibits record-high transverse thermoelectric generation performance and permanent magnet features.

title: Multi-physics mechanisms and regulation of perovskite grain boundaries: insights into carrier dynamics, ion migration, thermodynamics, and thermal stress
abstract: This study investigates the multi-physics mechanisms of grain boundaries in perovskite solar cells by performing opto-electro-thermal-stress coupled simulations, with analyses from carrier transport, ion migration, thermodynamics, and thermal stress.

title: Eliminating high-dimensional defects by upward unidirectional crystallization for efficient and stable inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Upward unidirectional crystallization is achieved by maximizing the difference in nucleation driving force between top and bottom sides of perovskites, which contributes to efficient and stable solar cells with eliminated high-dimensional defects.

title: Melamine holding PbI 2 with three “arms”: an effective chelation strategy to control the lead iodide to perovskite conversion for inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Herein, we have proposed a novel tridentate chelation strategy to manage the excessive amount of unreacted PbI 2 in perovskite films, achieving a inverted PSC device with a PCE of 25.66% by sequential deposition method.

title: Direct visualization and mechanistic insights into initial lithium plating in anode-free lithium metal batteries
abstract: Mechanistic understanding of internal pressure differences in anode-free Li metal batteries and their impact on Li distribution and morphology evolution on Cu electrodes during initial Li plating, visualised by ToF-SIMS mapping and PFIB-SEM imaging.

title: Blade printing of low-melting-point alloys as back electrodes for high-efficiency and stable inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Low-melting-point alloys are introduced as innovative electrodes for inverted perovskite solar cells (PSCs) PSCs with In–Sn–Bi electrodes fabricated by doctor printing achieved a record efficiency of 22.48% and exhibited high stability in air.

title: High-entropy-induced CoO 6 octahedral distortion for boosted oxygen evolution reaction at high temperature
abstract: The CoO 6 octahedral distortion induced by A-site high-entropy engineering enhances lattice oxygen activity and boosts high-temperature oxygen evolution reaction performance in solid oxide electrolysis cells.

title: Non-destructive degradation pattern decoupling for early battery trajectory prediction via physics-informed learning
abstract: The paper proposes a physics-informed model to predict battery lifetime trajectories by computing thermodynamic and kinetic parameters, saving costly data that has not been established for sustainable manufacturing, reuse, and recycling.

title: Anode-free sodium metal batteries: optimisation of electrolytes and interphases
abstract: The electrolyte and interphase chemistry plays a significant role in anode-free sodium metal batteries. Progresses in electrolyte optimization and interphase engineering are herein reviewed, focusing on their collective performance improvement.

title: Unraveling the interfacial homogeneity and bulk crystallization for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells via ionic liquids
abstract: Achieving the interfacial homogeneity and bulk crystallization for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells via ionic liquids.

title: Digitally-assisted structure design of a large-size proton exchange membrane fuel cell
abstract: A digitally-assisted method is proposed to accelerate the structure design of large-size proton exchange membrane fuel cells, including backward engineering and forward design.

title: Deciphering the interplay between tin vacancies and free carriers in the ion transport of tin-based perovskites
abstract: In this work, we demonstrate that a high concentration of hole carriers and tin vacancies facilitates the migration of iodide, enhancing ionic conductivity and mobile ion density in Sn-based halide perovskites.

title: Zinc–bromine batteries revisited: unlocking liquid-phase redox chemistry for next-generation energy storage
abstract: This review highlights recent progress in zinc–bromine batteries, including advances in anode and cathode materials, electrolyte engineering, and interface design, offering insights for next-generation energy storage systems.

title: Induced oriented attachment of a ZnO electron transport layer enables over 20% efficiency in solution-processed conventional organic solar cells
abstract: Dual-functional solid additives induce oriented attachment of ZnO nanoparticles, simultaneously regulating nanoparticle ordering and defect passivation, enabling record efficiencies in both rigid and flexible organic solar cells.

title: A solvation-driven reevaluation of organic electrolytes for zinc batteries
abstract: Organic electrolytes based on mass-produced components mitigate calendar-life deficiencies in Zn batteries while minimizing levelized cost.

title: Computationally accelerated discovery of mixed metal compounds for chemical looping combustion and beyond
abstract: High-throughput computational screening and machine learning accelerate the rational design of mixed metal compounds for diverse chemical looping applications, transforming materials discovery from trial-and-error to data-driven approaches.

title: Cobalt metal enables ultrahigh-efficiency, long-life, and dendrite-free aqueous multivalent batteries
abstract: Cobalt metal exhibits an exceptional plating performance, including ultrahigh efficiency (99.9%), long cycling (4000 hours, 5.5 months), and micro-spherical morphology. It can be paired with various cathodes into full cell applications.

title: A review on organic nanoparticle-based optoelectronic devices: from synthesis to applications
abstract: An overview of water/alcohol-based organic nanoparticles applied in optoelectronic devices, encompassing the entire journey from nanoparticle synthesis to practical applications.

title: Over one-micron-thick void-free perovskite layers enable highly efficient and fully printed solar cells
abstract: Guided by phase-field simulations, a pre-coated 2D perovskite layer enables the growth of void-free perovskite layers over one-micron-thick, achieving high-efficiency, fully printed solar cells.

title: Amorphous material based heterostructures with disordered heterointerfaces for advanced rechargeable batteries
abstract: The recent developments of amorphous material based heterostructures with disordered heterointerfaces for advanced rechargeable batteries are reviewed, focusing on the relation between material structure and electrochemical performance.

title: A dynamically assembled bionic ion pump interface towards high-rate and stable-cycling zinc metal batteries
abstract: A bionic ion pump interface is developed by introducing acetylated proteins (α-HP ace ) with strong Zn 2+ recognition ability.

title: Double amidino-mediated multiple hydrogen-bonded Dion–Jacobson perovskites enable oriented crystallization for efficient inverted FAPbI 3 solar cells and modules (642 cm 2 )
abstract: A double amidino-based derivative, namely PhDFACl 2 , has been explored as a spacer for DJ 2D/3D FAPbI 3 -based perovskites. Champion efficiencies of 26.10%, 24.81%, and 18.20% for 0.1 cm 2 , 1.01 cm 2 , and 642 cm 2 devices have been achieved.

title: Breaking the symmetry of interfacial molecules with push–pull substituents enables 19.67% efficiency organic solar cells featuring enhanced charge extraction
abstract: The unsymmetrical push–pull strategy in the interfacial layer enables fast charge extraction and suppresses surface and trap-assisted recombination, achieving a 19.67% efficiency in binary single junction organic solar cells.

title: Decoupling electrode kinetics to elucidate reaction mechanisms in alkaline water electrolysis
abstract: Alkaline water electrolysis provides a scalable and cost-effective route for hydrogen production. This work uncovers distinct HER/OER kinetics and introduces a dual-sensing strategy to guide catalyst design and enhance system performance.

title: Robust interphase derived from a dual-cation ionic liquid electrolyte enabling exceptional stability for nickel-rich layered cathodes
abstract: A dual-cation-based ionic liquid electrolyte with NaPF 6 additive, was designed for nickel-rich cathodes in Li-metal battery. The formed uniform and robust electrode/electrolyte interphases enables the exceptional 1500-cycle life of Li||NCM83 cells.

title: Crystal plane shielding and D-band modulation synergistically achieve durable (100) textured zinc anodes
abstract: Reconstruction of Zn surfaces using BED and its dynamic adsorption–desorption behavior synergistically improve the stability of Zn anodes.

title: Hierarchically porous carbon supports enable efficient syngas production in electrified reactive capture
abstract: Hierarchical carbon supports, internally coated with PDA and catalyst, enhance reactant mass transport, achieve molecular dispersion of the catalyst, and tune the electronic environment of the Co center.

title: Synergy of phase and interface engineering of manganese difluoride enables high-efficiency potassium-ion batteries
abstract: By adjusting the synthetic conditions, ultrafine rutile-phase MnF 2 nanoparticles are uniformly embedded into carbon nanofibers, which induce the formation of robust KF-rich SEI layers, thereby achieving excellent potassium storage performance.

title: Direct seawater electrolysis for green hydrogen production: electrode designs, cell configurations, and system integrations
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive analysis of design strategies and future challenges related to long-term stability (electrode, cell, and system) in direct seawater electrolysis (DSE).

title: Dual-site passivation by heterocycle functionalized amidinium cations toward high-performance inverted perovskite solar cells and modules
abstract: This work reports a dual-site passivation of anionic and cationic defects through heterocycle functionalized amidinium cations for achieving highly efficient perovskite solar cells.

title: Efficient hydrogen evolution at Ni/CeO x interfaces in anion-exchange membrane water electrolysers
abstract: Simultaneous reduction of concentration and activation overpotentials at hierarchically porous Ni/CeO x interfaces in anion-exchange membrane water electrolysers.

title: Enhancing the kinetics and reversibility of copper batteries via anionic chemistry
abstract: The effect of anions on the reversibility of Cu electrodes has been systematically investigated. Perchlorate (ClO 4 − ) demonstrates the best reversibility and fastest plating/stripping kinetics by inhibiting the formation of the Cu 2 O by-product.

title: Engineering an ion-pumping solid electrolyte interphase for ultra-stable aqueous zinc-ion batteries under deep discharge conditions
abstract: A novel graphene oxide-enhanced poly(ether- block -amide) artificial solid electrolyte interphase enables directional Zn 2+ pumping via functional group-electronegativity synergy, promoting uniform deposition and dendrite suppression.

title: Enhanced high-temperature energy storage in semi-aromatic polyimides via dual regulation of short-range ordered and crosslinked architectures
abstract: By the dual regulation of the short-range ordered structure and the cross-linked structure, semi-aromatic polyimide (E-SAPI 260 ) attained a high U d of 8.61 J cm −3 and 6.50 J cm −3 at 150 °C and 200 °C, respectively, with η exceeding 90%.

title: High-power hydrogel-based moisture-electric generators
abstract: This work presents a high-power hydrogel-based moisture-electric generator via molecular engineering design, which achieves a superior open-circuit voltage of 0.6 V and a short-circuit current of 0.58 mA cm −2 .

title: Polarity coupling in biphasic electrolytes enables iodine/polyiodide co-extraction for portable Zn–iodine batteries following a liquid–liquid conversion route
abstract: A biphasic (BP) electrolyte system composed of two immiscible solutions of Zn(TFSI) 2 in EA and ZnSO 4 /KI in water is designed to address the key issues existing in Zn–iodine batteries.

title: Phase-stabilized GeTe with optimized interfaces for high-performance thermoelectric energy conversion
abstract: Complete stabilization of cubic GeTe by Mn–Sb co-doping combined with optimized Co diffusion barriers yields robust thermoelectric modules with a peak efficiency of 12.2%, addressing long-standing phase and interface challenges.

title: Pushing slope- to plateau-type behavior in hard carbon for sodium-ion batteries via local structure rearrangement
abstract: Pushing slope- to plateau-type behavior is related to the transition from a critical interlayer distance to an average crystallite width of graphitic domain control, and is limited by the closed pore volume of hard carbon.

title: Thermosensitive slurry electrolyte design for efficient electrochemical heat harvesting
abstract: A thermoelectrochemical cell for efficient waste heat harvesting is presented, utilizing a thermosensitive slurry electrolyte to co-optimize the temperature coefficient ( α ), specific charge capacity ( q c ) and specific heat capacity ( c p ).

title: A review on low-concentration electrolytes for rechargeable batteries
abstract: Due to their lower cost, better wettability, and more salt options, low concentration electrolytes (LCEs) have inspired numerous interests. This review outlines the current knowledge on the design and application of LCEs in rechargeable batteries.

title: Revolutionizing wearable sustainable energy enabled by mechano-electric conversion fibers
abstract: Wearable electronics face energy and sensing challenges. Mechano-electric conversion fibers transform body energy into electricity and create self-sustaining energy systems, enhancing the practicality and multifunctionality of wearable devices.

title: Elucidating lithium-ion diffusion kinetics in cation-disordered rocksalt cathodes
abstract: Dependency of lithium diffusion kinetics on the site energy variations in DRX. In DRX, lithium ions occupying larger-volume octahedral sites exhibit more unstable site energies. This instability serves as an activation barrier.

title: Trace molecular chelation engineering of a self-healing hybrid interphase for highly stable aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Molecular chelation-driven interface engineering employs polar groups to lower water activity by H-bond reorganization and Zn 2+ chelation, forming a PSZ/inorganic SEI hybrid layer that enables adaptive corrosion protection and suppressed degradation.

title: A ruthenium–titania core–shell nanocluster catalyst for efficient and durable alkaline hydrogen evolution
abstract: Research strategy and synthesis of a core–shell Ru catalyst.

title: Achieving highly efficient, mechanically robust and thermally stable organic solar cells through optimizing the branching position and side chain length of small molecule acceptors
abstract: Highly efficient, mechanically robust, and thermally stable OSCs have been developed by optimizing the branching position and side chain length of SMAs, marking a significant advancement in the field of intrinsically stretchable OSCs.

title: Modeling carbon-free energy conversion systems: enhanced hydrazine-assisted hydrogen production with dual-electric-field effect on needle-like Ru/CoP catalysts
abstract: The local electric field weakens the hydrogen-bond network and enhances H 2 O transport. The built-in electric field creates electron-deficient sites, improving the adsorption of N 2 H 4 . Finally, a self-powered zero-carbon energy system was built.

title: Breaking the limits of Ruddlesden–Popper cathodes to achieve a game-changer for proton-conducting solid oxide fuel cells
abstract: The high-entropy R–P cathode, La 0.4 Pr 0.4 Nd 0.4 Ba 0.4 Sr 0.4 NiO 4+ x (LPNBSN), merges record-breaking performance with exceptional durability for H-SOFCs—a critical leap toward sustainable energy technologies.

title: Self-supported NiTe@NiMo electrodes enabling efficient sulfion oxidation reaction toward energy-saving and chlorine-free hybrid seawater electrolysis at high current densities
abstract: NiTe@NiMo/NF electrodes show outstanding electrocatalytic performance for sulfion oxidation in seawater, able to stably operate at a high current density under a low voltage, simultaneously producing H 2 and sulfur in an energy-saving manner.

title: Design principles for 3D thermoelectric materials in power generators
abstract: We propose a universal theoretical framework for 3D thermoelectric leg design that integrates geometry, material properties, and boundary conditions. Experimental validation with printed (Bi,Sb) 2 Te 3 demonstrates up to 466% efficiency improvement.

title: Optimal selection of RuO 2 for durable oxygen evolution reactions in acidic media by continuous regulation of Ru–O covalency
abstract: We obtained excellent activity and stability at high current densities by continuously doping Cr while successively modulating the electron transfer capacity of the catalyst as well as the Ru–O covalency.

title: 19.46%-Efficiency all-polymer organic solar cells with excellent outdoor operating stability enabled by active layer reconstruction
abstract: Layer-by-layer blade deposition was employed along with a multi-component strategy to construct a quaternary all-polymer system, achieving a high power conversion efficiency of 19.46% and improved outdoor operating stability.

title: An integrated design for high-energy, durable zinc–iodine batteries with ultra-high recycling efficiency
abstract: This work presents a zinc–iodine battery featuring a liquid–liquid biphasic electrolyte and an integrated cell structure, which facilitates an iodine loading of 69.8 mg cm −2 , a self-discharge rate of 3.4% per month, and ∼100% recycling efficiency.

title: One-stone-two-birds: over 26% efficiency in perovskite solar cells via synergistic crystallization & interface regulation
abstract: Parabanic acid serves to concurrently align perovskite crystal growth and passivate interface defects, enabling highly efficient and stable perovskite solar cells.

title: Progress and perspectives on hydrogen storage and release in the negative hydrogen medium
abstract: This review presents the design principles and related mechanism studies of catalyst for hydrogen storage and cyclic regeneration of the negative hydrogen medium, providing valuable guidance for the development of hydrogen energy.

title: Suppressed surface lattice vacancies and distortion through lattice anchoring for efficient FAPbI 3 perovskite quantum dot solar cells
abstract: A facile surface lattice anchoring strategy is reported to stabilize the surface lattice of perovskite quantum dots, which could substantially improve their optoelectronic properties and crystal stabilities for application in solar cells.

title: Efficient charge separation at localized 2D ferroelectric domains in perovskite solar cells
abstract: Ferroelectric properties can be utilized for efficient charge carrier separation by spontaneous electric polarization.

title: Electrolyte tailoring and interfacial engineering for safe and high-temperature lithium-ion batteries
abstract: By replacing the inert, strongly positive electrostatic potential of TTE with the active, weakly positive electrostatic potential of MDFSA, thermally resistant interfacial films are formed, enhancing the battery's performance at high temperatures.

title: Amorphous indium–zinc oxide layer with transport and protective dual-function for high-efficiency semitransparent and tandem Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 solar cells
abstract: Bifunctional IZO was soft-sputtered for semitransparent Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 solar cells, achieving the highest efficiency both in semitransparent and all-antimony-based tandem devices.

title: Insight into the corrosion microcell and passivation mechanisms of a Zn anode for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A spontaneous microcell corrosion mechanism of Zn anode in a static ZnSO 4 solution was revealed via localized electrochemical analysis techniques. Zn corrosion could induce its self-passivation, causing the formation of plate-like Zn 4 SO 4 (OH) 6 · x H 2 O.

title: Ligand effects enhancing low-temperature oxygen reduction kinetics in neutral conditions
abstract: The local PO microenvironment activates the adjacent C atom to modulate the oxygen adsorption through the Yeager model, thereby enhancing the ORR kinetics even in hostile environments with low temperatures and proton concentrations.

title: Salt dissociation and localized high-concentration solvation at the interface of a fluorinated gel and polymer solid electrolyte
abstract: The interfaces between fluorinated gel solid electrolyte (FG-SE) and PVHF-SPE form fast ion transport pathways with high lithium salt dissociation and a local high-concentration (LHC) solvation structure.

title: Two-terminal perovskite/Cu(In,Ga)Se 2 tandems with conformal coatings based on commercial bottom cells with >26% efficiency
abstract: Employing DMF-assisted I-2PACz reconstruction and a BSSS strategy improves conformal SAM/perovskite coverage and C60 adsorption, enabling a record 26.14% (certified 25.21%) tandem efficiency on commercial CIGS.

title: Degradation phenomena in PEMWE revealed by correlative electrochemical and nanostructure analysis
abstract: Correlative analysis reveals operational mode-dependent PEMWE degradation, highlighting Pt dissolution and Ir oxidation as key durability challenges.

title: Fine-tuning the hierarchical morphology of multi-component organic photovoltaics via a dual-additive strategy for 20.5% efficiency
abstract: The dual-additive strategy forms balanced crystallization and phase separation, with a record efficiency of 20.52% (certified 19.92%) achieved.

title: Unraveling the dynamic transformation of azobenzene-driven redox electrolytes for Zn-ion hybrid capacitors
abstract: This study investigates the behavior of azobenzene-driven redox electrolytes for hybrid capacitors, highlighting the synergistic interactions of redox-active molecules and providing fundamental insights into electrochemical interphase transformations.

title: Identifying the role of Zn self-dissolution in the anode corrosion process in Zn-ion batteries
abstract: The degradation and corrosion of zinc metal anodes in aqueous electrolytes is severe, even when at rest. We uncover that the self-dissolution of zinc, and the ensuing change in local pH, is a necessary prerequisite for corrosion product formation.

title: Unveiling capacity limitations of MnO 2 in rechargeable Zn chemistry
abstract: MnO 2 dissolution during first discharge in neutral Zn–MnO 2 batteries originates preferentially from Mn–Mn edge-sharing coordination.

title: Achieving a stable 518 Wh kg −1 Li metal pouch cell via SEI reconstruction engineering for high Li + conductive hetero-grain boundaries
abstract: A SEI reconstruction method on Li anodes via perfluoroether carboxylic acid spraying, yielding a LiF/Li 2 O-rich SEI with high Li + conductive hetero-grain boundaries, enabling a 5.8 Ah pouch cell to achieve 518 Wh kg −1 and 100 cycles.

title: An unwanted guest in the electrochemical oxidation of high-voltage Li-ion battery electrolytes: the life of highly reactive protons
abstract: Electrochemical oxidation of Li-ion electrolytes generates highly reactive protons that trigger cascading degradation of solvents, salts, and cathodes, revealing a critical origin of battery performance loss.

title: Cathode passivation suppression enables ultrastable industry-leading alkaline water electrolysis at hundred-ampere currents
abstract: Ru nanoparticles reconstruct water networks on RANEY® Ni, decreasing local pH near cathode to inhibit passivation. The cathode achieves 10 A cm −2 at 2.3 V with 2500 hours stability at 1.0 A cm −2 .

title: Electrolytic gold plating, stripping, and ion transport dynamics through a solid-state iodide perovskite
abstract: The diffusion coefficient (10 −11 to 10 −10 cm 2 s −1 ) and ion concentration (10 15 to 10 17 cm −3 ) for Au i + in MAPbI 3 were quantified via diffusion modelling. Gold species can be reversibly plated on or stripped from the cathode electrolytically.

title: Self-assembled porous salt crystals for solar-powered crystallization
abstract: Leveraging the self-amplifying salt creeping and efflorescence effects, the salt crystals self-assemble to form a hierarchical porous salt evaporator, enabling passive liquid supply and efficient evaporation.

title: Triphenylamine–ethylenedioxythiophene copolymers for perovskite solar cells: impact of substituent type and alternation
abstract: A copolymer of triphenylamine and ethylenedioxythiophene affords stable perovskite solar cells with an average efficiency of 25.4%.

title: Rapid electrothermal rejuvenation of spent lithium cobalt oxide cathode
abstract: A rapid electrothermal method heals spent cathodes. With heteroatom doping, the rejuvenated cathodes show improved performance at 4.6 V. This low-cost, eco-friendly method supports sustainable high-performance lithium-ion battery supply.

title: Compacting surface charge layers for efficient charge transfer toward stable Zn anodes
abstract: An electron transfer-mediated EDL compression mechanism effectively shortens Zn 2+ transport paths and reduces hydrolysis through hydrogen bond confinement to H 2 O, achieving ultra-long cycling of over 1000 h at 10 mA cm −2 .

title: A weakly solvating solvent-based quasi-solid electrolyte for sodium metal batteries
abstract: A novel weakly solvating solvent-based quasi-solid electrolyte (WS-QSE) using ring ethers for sodium batteries achieves superior cycling performance through a designed anion-dominated sodium ion solvation structure.

title: A self-healing non-precious metal oxide anode in proton exchange membrane electrolysis beyond 1000 h stability at 2 A cm −2
abstract: This work demonstrates the dissolution–redeposition dynamic equilibrium of non-precious metal anode catalysts under PEMWE conditions, establishing stable active sites and achieving unprecedented long-term stability at ultrahigh current densities.

title: Solid-state n-type thermodiffusion-assisted thermogalvanic cells with unprecedented thermal energy conversion
abstract: High thermopower solid-state n-type thermodiffusion-assisted thermogalvanic cells were developed by designing a polymer complex—combining a polyelectrolyte with a galvanic couple—to enable constructive thermodiffusion and thermogalvanic effects.

title: A highly elastic and Li-ion conductive binder enables stable operation of silicon microparticle anodes in high-capacity and high-energy-density pouch cells
abstract: A highly elastic and Li-ion conductive binder is designed for addressing adhesive failure in Si microparticle anodes. The high-capacity pouch cells exhibit exceptional cycling stability, highlighting the practical viability of the proposed binder.

title: Soft conjugation extension strategy of self-assembled molecules for achieving efficient and mechanically stable flexible perovskite solar cells
abstract: SAMs with soft conjugation extension enhance the electrical and mechanical contacts of ITO/SAMs/perovskite structures, thus achieving efficient and mechanically stable f-PSCs.

title: Designing next-generation all-weather and efficient atmospheric water harvesting powered by solar energy
abstract: We propose a next-generation solar-powered hybrid atmospheric water harvesting system for all-weather and efficient water production worldwide, which leverages the advantages of complementary AWH mechanisms with synergistic integration of heat pump.

title: Surface-confined growth of Ru amorphous sub-nanoclusters on reductive Mn 3 O 4 : a strongly coupled interface engineering for efficient neutral hydrogen production
abstract: The Ru-ASNs provides a proliferation of active sites and modifies the local electron distribution, thereby forming a strongly coupled interface with Mn 3 O 4 . The obtained interfacial electrocatalyst exhibits excellent performance of neutral HER.

title: Buried interface engineering enables efficient and refurbished CsPbI 3 perovskite quantum dot solar cells
abstract: Interfacial engineering has proven to be extremely important for colloidal quantum dot (QD) solar cells.

title: Polymeric membranes in carbon capture, utilization, and storage: current trends and future directions in decarbonization of industrial flue gas and climate change mitigation
abstract: Material, process, and computational developments for polymeric membrane-assisted decarbonization.

title: Advancing extreme-temperature-tolerant zinc–air batteries through photothermal transition metal sulfide heterostructures
abstract: Utilizing the photothermal assistance principle provides fascinating opportunities to explore the connection between the catalyst photothermal effect and zinc–air battery characteristics under normal to extreme conditions.

title: A lithium-selective “OR-gate” enables fast-kinetics and ultra-stable Li-rich cathodes for polymer-based solid-state batteries
abstract: A negative OR gate on the surface of lithium rich layered oxide enhances the transport of Li + , making it an ideal choice for solid polymer electrolyte systems.

title: A large conjugated rigid dimer acceptor enables 20.19% efficiency in organic solar cells
abstract: A dimer acceptor QD-1 with a large conjugated rigid skeleton is reported, showing low disorder, weak electro-photon coupling, and thus low non-radiative recombination loss. An efficiency of 20.19% is achieved for the QD-1-based ternary device.

title: Magneto-conversion anode design for unlocking high energy density and dendrite-free hybrid lithium–ion/lithium–metal batteries
abstract: A magnetically tailored conversion strategy integrates ferromagnetic oxides with an external magnetic field to regulate Li + flux and homogenize nucleation barriers, thereby enabling dendrite-free Li deposition and enhancing energy density.

title: Oscillating lithium ion-acceptor fluorine-donor electrolytes for practical fast-charging high-energy lithium metal pouch cells
abstract: An oscillating Li + -acceptor fluorine-donor electrolyte incorporating a new co-solvent with an asymmetric super-lithiophilic fluorine group resolves the dilemma between fast electrolyte Li + transport and stable interphases.

title: Li-ion nanorobots with enhanced mobility for fast-ion conducting polymer electrolytes
abstract: The activated hopping of ultrasmall nanoparticles, in conjunction with the accelerated segmental motion of the polymer, establishes a dual-channel Li + transport pathway that significantly enhances the Li + conductivity of the polymer electrolyte.

title: Lean-solvent solid electrolytes for safer and more durable lithium batteries: a crucial review
abstract: This review thoroughly examines the impact of lean-solvent solid electrolyte (LSEs) on for safer and more durable lithium batteries. It also provides a comprehensive overview of existing LSEs.

title: Revisiting the interfacial chemistry of calcium metal anodes: the importance of inorganic-rich solid/electrolyte interfaces derived from an aggregation-dominated electrolyte
abstract: The degradation of Ca anode in ester/ether electrolytes is attached to an organic-rich SEI. On this basis, we propose a desirable Ca 2+ solvation sheath rich in AGG and obtain inorganic-rich SEI, achieving high reversible Ca 2+ plating/striping.

title: Investigating the effect of lithiation on polycrystalline NMC811 Li-ion battery cathode cracking using in situ SEM micromechanical testing
abstract: The mechanical degradation of polycrystalline NMC811 cathode particles during electrochemical cycling was investigated using powder compression and nanoindentation in situ SEM.

title: Achieving unprecedented power-output in 4-terminal mirror-symmetrical printable carbon CsPbBr 3 solar cells through dual-solvent engineering
abstract: Tyndall effect-guided dual-solvent engineering optimized printable carbon-based CsPbBr 3 solar cells, achieving a record PCE of 10.18%. A 4-terminal concentrator enhanced output to 29.44 mW cm −2 , with 93.2% PCE retention after 1000 hours at 150 °C.

title: Zinc chemistry regulated by chitosan-based poly(aprotic/protic ionic liquid)s with multi-anion–cation interactions for highly reversible Zn-ion batteries
abstract: The unique multi-anion–cation interaction mechanism is established in the well-designed CPAPILs-B additive through a mild protonation reaction for highly reversible Zn-ion batteries.

title: Hydrophobic ionic liquid enabled polyiodide confined transport in a cathode, realizing high areal capacity, stable zinc–iodine batteries
abstract: A liquid ionic conductive agent for an iodine cathode improves the conductivity of thick electrodes and prevents polyiodide shuttle effects, thereby leading to achievement of aqueous zinc–iodine batteries with high loading and high areal capacity.

title: Suppressed non-radiative loss and efficient hole transfer at a small highest occupied molecular orbital offset endows binary organic solar cells with 19.73% efficiency and a small efficiency-cost gap
abstract: Two novel acceptors are designed and synthesized, and the PTQ11:PEH-F binary system is highly promising for industrial cost-effective organic photovoltaics.

title: Developing an electro-chemo-mechanically synergistic effect via the cholesteric cellulose crystalline interphase for highly stable flexible zinc metal batteries
abstract: A cholesteric nanocellulose (C-CNC) film balanced Zn 2+ transport and redox kinetics, and homogenized electric and ionic fields via electro-chemo-mechanical synergy, thereby enhancing the stability and reversibility of the Zn metal anode.

title: A “grafting technique” to tailor the interfacial behavior of hard carbon anodes for stable sodium-ion batteries
abstract: A novel strategy for grafting a highly fluorinated molecule on the HC surface (FHC), which functionally enhances the reversible sodium storage behavior in the slope region and contributes to the architecture of a robust NaF-rich SEI.

title: Activating Ni atoms up to the third nearest neighbor around single-atom Fe into highly active sites for PGM-free anion-exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: Fe single-atom doping activates up to the third nearest-neighbor Ni atoms into active sites for efficient PGM-free AEMFCs.

title: Dual-anion ionic liquid electrolytes: a strategy for achieving high stability and conductivity in lithium metal batteries
abstract: The dual-anion ionic liquid electrolyte (D-LCILE) significantly improves lithium-ion mobility and SEI stability, leading to >99.93% capacity retention over 200 cycles in lithium metal full cells.

title: Surface-tension-induced high packing density carbon nanoribbon films with ultrahigh volumetric capacitance
abstract: The internal hollow voids of tubular carbon fibers are eliminated due to the surface-tension-induced structural shrinkage, leading to high porosity and dense stacking. The obtained electrodes are suitable for ultrafast-charging supercapacitors.

title: Interface diagnostics platform for thin-film solid-state batteries
abstract: Understanding the impedances of battery materials remains a major challenge. Features of Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (EIS) can now be accurately attributed using a new materials-agnostic methodology.

title: Comment on “Boosting the solar water oxidation performance of a BiVO 4 photoanode by crystallographic orientation control” by H. S. Han, S. Shin, D. H. Kim, I. J. Park, J. S. Kim, P. Huang, J. Lee, I. S. Cho and X. Zheng, Energy Environ. Sci ., 2018, 11 , 1299
abstract: The presence of scarce (101)-oriented F:SnO 2 grains in the fluorine-doped SnO 2 layer with a slight (100) texture seems insufficient to drive predominantly (001)-oriented growth of a BiVO 4 photoanode through the alleged microscale epitaxy.

title: Upcycling spent medium-Ni cathodes via novel liquified salt sourcing
abstract: A novel liquified-salts-assisted upcycling strategy achieves compositional and morphological upgrading of spent cathodes, offering a scalable pathway for high-performance cathode regeneration with reduced process energy and material waste.

title: 26.35%-Efficiency and high-bifaciality n-TOPCon solar cells enabled by UV-ps laser-induced selective modification of double-layered SiO x /n + -poly-Si passivating contacts
abstract: Double-layered SiO x /n + -poly-Si films with laterally modulated thickness applied in TOPCon solar cells can reduced recombination, enhance contact quality, decrease risk of paste burn-through of the passivation layer and reduce parasitic absorption.

title: Surface-deprotonated ultra-small SnO 2 quantum dots for high-performance perovskite solar cells
abstract: SnO 2 electron transport layers (ETLs) have significantly boosted the recent record efficiencies in perovskite solar cells (PSCs).

title: Zn 2+ -blocking effects of a proton-rich polyaniline layer enable Ah-level Zn–MnO 2 batteries
abstract: Herein, we introduced a PANI layer to provide a proton-rich micro-environment for an organic modified MnO 2 cathode featuring Mn–N bonds, enabling selective H + insertion rather than Zn 2+ and reversible MnO 2 /Mn 2+ redox for advanced Zn–MnO 2 batteries.

title: Molecular integration of Lewis bases for efficient and stable inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: A molecular integration strategy is proposed to identify a novel yet efficient Lewis base for effective defect passivation, thereby enabling the realization of high-performance p–i–n perovskite solar cells.

title: A universal surface fixed charge reconstruction strategy to minimize contact loss in wide bandgap perovskite photovoltaics
abstract: The “healing–reconstruction” strategy results in decreased V OC loss, with V OC values of 1.24, 1.37, and 1.44 V for 1.65, 1.79, and 1.91 eV perovskites, respectively.

title: The redox aspects of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: We are considering fundamental questions of how redox reactions take place and charge is conducted in redox solids. Redox properties of electrode materials can be explained by considering the Nernst equations for homogeneous or segregated materials.
